commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed for the compatibility and session-history ownership choices; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this pass.; Cleared: The diff does not add dependencies, workflows, install scripts, or credential handling, and it explicitly keeps sensitive media out of durable transcript mirrors. (sha=088b030cf073f8731b74354b202faf0c17792f57)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Gateway internal UI source-reply broadcasting and transcript mirroring for TUI/internal chat clients, preserves the legacy `webchat` message-tool channel, and updates docs plus focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review: current docs and TUI registration establish the expected internal UI behavior, while current main only blocks external route inheritance for WebChat clients. The PR body also includes exact-head live TUI/Gateway proof, though I did not rerun it in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed for the compatibility and session-history ownership choices; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this pass.

target.title: fix(gateway): deliver internal source replies to TUI
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix message-tool source replies from internal OpenClaw chat surfaces so TUI/WebChat receive the visible reply in the active run instead of inheriting a stale external delivery route.
- Keep the legacy tool-result/action channel as `webchat` for compatibility, and use `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"` as the internal sink marker.
- Keep external channels such as Telegram, Slack, and Discord as separate deliverable channels; this does not make their conversations visible in WebChat/TUI.
- Split the source-reply display/mirror path into focused gateway helpers, add coverage for current-run routing, and remove PR-introduced dead exports (`isTuiClient`, `GatewayCurrentSessionClientId`, `GatewayInternalChatSurfaceClientId`).

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #38957

## Design Boundary

This PR intentionally does not create a new public channel, config key, CLI flag, provider policy, or external delivery path. `internal-ui` is an internal sink marker for message-tool source replies on OpenClaw-owned chat surfaces such as TUI, WebChat, Control UI, and native UI mode.

The compatibility boundary is that `MessageActionRunResult.channel` and the model-facing result payload `channel` stay `webchat`. Existing agents, workflows, downstream parsers, and source copies that key on the historical message-tool `channel` value continue to see the old route label. New code should read `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"` when it needs to know that the visible source reply was delivered into the OpenClaw-owned internal UI sink.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI-originated `message_tool_only` source replies render in the active TUI/current-run chat even when the session has a stale external delivery route, and the tool result keeps `channel: "webchat"` while identifying the visible sink with `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"`.

Real environment tested: Live local dev host on exact PR head `088b030cf073f8731b74354b202faf0c17792f57` (`OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (088b030)`), isolated staged OpenClaw home at `/tmp/openclaw-test-home-crK8kR`, foreground Gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, throwaway loopback token auth, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, `gateway.controlUi.enabled: false`, bundled plugins from this worktree, live `openai/gpt-5.5` / `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, seeded session route `lastChannel: "telegram"` and `lastTo: "6101296751"` before the TUI run.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
PROOF_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-test-home-crK8kR
SESSION=agent:main:tui-live-proof-terminal-202605201207

HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \
USERPROFILE="$PROOF_HOME" \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET=1 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18789 \
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run \
  --port 18789 \
  --bind loopback \
  --auth token \
  --token '<throwaway proof token>' \
  --allow-unconfigured \
  --ws-log compact

HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \
USERPROFILE="$PROOF_HOME" \
node scripts/run-node.mjs tui \
  --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789 \
  --token '<throwaway proof token>' \
  --session "$SESSION" \
  --timeout-ms 180000
```

In the real terminal TUI PTY, I typed and submitted:

```text
Live routing proof from the real terminal TUI. You must use the message tool, not ordinary final text. Send exactly this text to the current TUI chat run: TUI TERMINAL LIVE PROOF TUI-TERM-1779278877-26056. No extra words, no markdown, no quotes.
```

I also ran a TUI-mode WebSocket proof client (`client.id: "openclaw-tui"`, `mode: "ui"`) against a separate seeded stale-route session and then queried `chat.history` over the Gateway WebSocket.

Evidence after fix: The actual terminal TUI rendered this standalone assistant reply after the typed prompt:

```text
TUI TERMINAL LIVE PROOF TUI-TERM-1779278877-26056
```

The `chat.history` verifier for `agent:main:tui-live-proof-terminal-202605201207` returned:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:tui-live-proof-terminal-202605201207",
  "historyContainsExpected": true,
  "messageCount": 4,
  "hasSourceReplySink": true,
  "hasCurrentRunTarget": true,
  "expected": "TUI TERMINAL LIVE PROOF TUI-TERM-1779278877-26056"
}
```

The separate TUI-mode WebSocket proof used stale route `{ "lastChannel": "telegram", "lastTo": "6101296751" }`, skipped channels, and received a live chat final with run id `idem-tui-live-1779278321-8006:internal-source-reply:0`; its `chat.history` check returned `historyContainsExpected: true`.

Observed result after fix: The message-tool source reply was visible in the active terminal TUI, persisted into `chat.history`, and resolved to the current internal UI run despite the stale Telegram route and skipped external channels. The proof path would have had no working Telegram delivery fallback because channels were intentionally skipped.

What was not tested: Live Telegram/Slack/Discord delivery was not rerun because this PR does not change their external delivery paths. Control UI static assets were disabled in the proof config to avoid an unrelated local `ui:build` startup delay; the exercised surface was Gateway + terminal TUI + `chat.history`. The throwaway proof token and temp proof home were not committed.

Before evidence: Earlier base negative control on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834` reproduced the failure shape: the model used the `message` tool and the source reply was persisted with `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"`, but the active TUI returned to idle without rendering the standalone reply.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/utils/message-channel.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` (6 files, 175 tests passed)
- `codex review --base origin/main` (clean; also ran `pnpm tsgo:prod` successfully during review)
- Dead-code audit: `git grep -n "\bisTuiClient\b\|\bGatewayCurrentSessionClientId\b\|\bGatewayInternalChatSurfaceClientId\b" -- . ':!node_modules' ':!dist' ':!coverage' || true` returned no matches
- Exact-head live terminal TUI + Gateway proof on `088b030cf073f8731b74354b202faf0c17792f57`, described above

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: yes
- Config/env changes: no
- Public SDK/API changes: no
- External channel behavior changes: no
- Migration needed: no
target.author: rubencu
